subject: [PATCH 01/14] mm: decouple protnone helpers from CONFIG_NUMA_BALANCING

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary:
Gave Acked-by

Reviewer Mike Rapoport suggested rephrasing the description of pte_protnone() to accurately convey its meaning, instead of removing it entirely.
reviews.raw_body:
I think it's better to rephrase the original comment so that it'll actually
describe what pte_protnone() means rather than remove it altogether.

2026-05-04.patch_summary: This patch decouples the protnone helpers from CONFIG_NUMA_BALANCING by introducing a new configuration option, CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PTE_PROTNONE. This allows architectures that support present-but-inaccessible page table entries (protnone PTEs) to select this option independently of NUMA balancing. The six supported architectures are updated to select this option, and the infrastructure is made available for use beyond NUMA balancing, such as in userfaultfd working set tracking. No functional changes are introduced, but the protnone support is now decoupled from NUMA balancing.
